mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals a labor market shock that triggers recession fears, causing investors to price in lower future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power for 5-day returns in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of consumer credit (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates, real rates turn deeply negative, forcing equity valuations to expand as investors chase yield, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Real equity risk premiums do not compress when nominal rates are near zero and inflation rises, or forward returns are flat or negative despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real consumer credit balances (RSAFS) reflect expanding household purchasing power and confidence, which translates directly into higher corporate sales and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with credit expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which reduces corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral during confirmed industrial production downturns, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between strong employment growth (PAYEMS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicates structural labor market frictions that compress wage growth and limit consumer spending, ultimately capping equity upside and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal shows no predictive power for 60-day returns, or equity markets rally despite widening employment-unemployment gaps.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) lead corporate earnings growth by 2-3 quarters due to the construction and homebuilding supply chain multiplier, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as forward-looking investors price in the upswing.
failure_condition: Housing starts fail to lead equity returns by the specified horizon, or forward returns are negative during confirmed housing booms.
